codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly delivers all five contract assertions: (1) VAL-ETEXT-003 - agent revision creates canonical appagent-authored revisions via the handleTaskCompletion hook; (2) VAL-ETEXT-004 - live progress events with doc_id correlation; (3) VAL-CROSS-119 - end-to-end attribution preservation in history; (4) VAL-CROSS-120 - no worker authorship (only user/appagent as canonical authors); (5) VAL-CROSS-122 - idempotent mutation tracking preventing duplicates on renewal/retry. The worker followed the go-worker skill procedure, added comprehensive tests (9 runtime tests, 7 store tests, 1 type test), and all validators pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker created .factory/library/etext-agent-revision.md documenting the feature's architecture, idempotency mechanism, event vocabulary, and key design decisions. This is excellent practice that should be encouraged for complex features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: File .factory/library/etext-agent-revision.md created with comprehensive documentation covering VAL-ETEXT-003, VAL-ETEXT-004, VAL-CROSS-119, VAL-CROSS-120, VAL-CROSS-122 implementations.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation follows the established pattern of using 'INSERT OR IGNORE' for idempotent operations, matching the existing task submission pattern seen in the runtime store.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/store/etext.go:297-314 uses INSERT OR IGNORE for CreateAgentMutation, similar to how runtime store handles task creation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handleTaskCompletion hook pattern for feature-specific side effects is a clean extension point that keeps the runtime generic while allowing features to respond to task completion.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/runtime/runtime.go:336-395 - handleTaskCompletion is called after both executeWithToolLoop and executeWithProvider, with etext-specific logic cleanly separated.

summary: Feature passes review. The e-text appagent revision flow is fully implemented with prompt-driven canonical revisions, live progress events, idempotent mutation tracking, and correct user-vs-appagent attribution. All 17 new tests pass, the frontend integration is complete, and documentation was added to the library.
